claims.text: GDACS's report for Tropical Cyclone BAVI-26, covering 01 Jul 2026 00:00 UTC to 05 Jul 2026 06:00 UTC, assessed an overall Red alert with a maximum wind speed of 287 km/h (Category 5).
GDACS reported an exposed population of 1.1 million people in areas experiencing Category 1 or higher winds from Tropical Cyclone BAVI-26.

GDACS assigned Tropical Cyclone BAVI-26 a GDACS score of 2.5, citing potential for high humanitarian impact from wind, storm surge and rainfall given medium vulnerability in the Northern Mariana Islands.

description: A tropical disturbance east-southeast of the Marianas organized into Tropical Depression 09W on 1 July 2026 and was named Bavi as a tropical storm on 2 July. Bavi underwent rapid intensification and reached Category 5 super typhoon strength by 4 July with sustained winds near 287 km/h, prompting GDACS to issue an overall Red alert as the storm tracked through Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, with typhoon warnings in effect through its passage near Rota early in the week before it continued west toward the Philippines and East Asia.

impact.summary: As of 6 July 2026, GDACS continued to assess an overall Red alert for Super Typhoon Bavi covering the period 1-5 July 2026, with maximum sustained winds of 287 km/h (Category 5) and about 1.1 million people exposed to Category 1 or higher winds as the storm passed through the Guam and Northern Mariana Islands area.

location.region: Western North Pacific, passing near Rota between Guam and Saipan

name: Super Typhoon Bavi — Guam & Northern Mariana Islands
